title: Hampi (Vijayanagara)
text: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire in the 14th century. The Virupaksha temple on the Tungabhadra river remains an active place of worship. The site is known for its boulder-strewn landscape and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The stone chariot at the Vittala temple is one of its most photographed monuments.

title: Taj Mahal
text: The Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum in Agra commissioned by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan for Mumtaz Mahal. It was completed in the seventeenth century and is set within formal Mughal gardens. The monument is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and draws millions of visitors each year. Its symmetry and inlaid stonework are widely studied examples of Mughal architecture.

title: Timbuktu and the Sankore Mosque
text: Timbuktu in Mali was a center of Islamic learning and trans-Saharan trade. The Sankore Mosque was part of a renowned scholarly community that preserved thousands of manuscripts. The city is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Its earthen architecture is maintained through periodic community replastering.

title: Konark Sun Temple
text: The Konark Sun Temple in Odisha is designed as a colossal chariot of the sun god Surya. It was built in the thirteenth century and is famous for its carved stone wheels. The temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Much of the original structure is now in ruins but the artistry remains celebrated.

title: Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram)
text: Mahabalipuram is a coastal town in Tamil Nadu known for Pallava-era rock-cut temples and monuments. The Shore Temple overlooks the Bay of Bengal. The Descent of the Ganges is a large open-air bas-relief carved into granite. The group of monuments is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: Khajuraho Group of Monuments
text: The Khajuraho temples in Madhya Pradesh were built by the Chandela dynasty between the tenth and twelfth centuries. They are known for nagara-style architecture and intricate sculpture. The monuments are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Only a portion of the original temples survive today.

title: Angkor Wat
text: Angkor Wat is a temple complex in Cambodia originally built in the twelfth century for the Khmer Empire. It was first dedicated to the god Vishnu and later used as a Buddhist site. It is the largest religious monument in the world by land area. Angkor Wat appears on the national flag of Cambodia.

title: Petra
text: Petra is an ancient city in Jordan carved into rose-colored sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans. It was a major caravan trading hub in antiquity. The Treasury, or Al-Khazneh, is its best known facade. Petra is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a popular archaeological destination.

title: Machu Picchu
text: Machu Picchu is a fifteenth-century Inca citadel set high in the Andes mountains of Peru. It features dry-stone walls built without mortar. The site was brought to international attention in the early twentieth century. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most visited landmarks in South America.

title: Great Wall of China
text: The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across northern China over many centuries. Sections near Beijing were rebuilt during the Ming dynasty. The wall was constructed for border defense and control of trade routes. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a national symbol of China.
